     Run `pnpm --filter @aegiskit/scanner coverage:matrix:write` to regenerate. -->

# Aegis coverage matrix — what it detects, and what it does not

> **Aegis does not — and cannot — find every vulnerability.** No static analyzer can (it is undecidable in
> general), and any tool that claims to is dangerous: it breeds false confidence, the worst security
> outcome. Aegis instead detects a precise set of high-impact classes with a **zero-false-positive design**
> (the benchmark holds precision at 1.0), and tells you exactly what it leaves to secure design, code
> review, and other tools. This page is generated from the live rule registry, so it never drifts from what
> ships.

## How to read this

Every finding is one of three **methods**, ordered by strength of evidence:

- **Taint (dataflow)** — a proven source→sink path (the path is attached to the finding). Hard evidence.
- **Semantic** — structural AST / SQL-predicate / regex-complexity analysis. Hard evidence.
- **Heuristic (advisory)** — a low-noise pattern or name match that surfaces a spot to review. It is a
  prompt, not proof; these are reported at medium confidence and **do not fail CI by default**.

Built-in rules: **33** total — 9 taint, 11 semantic, 13 heuristic. Dynamic confirmation (below) can upgrade a static finding to "confirmed".

## Rules

| Rule | Detects | Severity | Method | OWASP |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| `authz/idor-tainted-scope` | Ownership filter bound to request-controlled input (IDOR) | HIGH | Taint (dataflow) | A01:2021 Broken Access Control |
| `authz/missing-access-filter` | Data query without a visible authorization check | HIGH | Heuristic (advisory) | A01:2021 Broken Access Control |
| `csp/nonce-minted-unused` | CSP nonce minted but never used | HIGH | Semantic | A05:2021 Security Misconfiguration |
| `csp/unsafe-inline` | CSP allows 'unsafe-inline'/'unsafe-eval' | HIGH | Heuristic (advisory) | A05:2021 Security Misconfiguration |
| `xss/dangerous-html-unsanitized` | dangerouslySetInnerHTML from an unsanitized source | HIGH | Heuristic (advisory) | A03:2021 Injection |
| `xss/tainted-dom-sink` | Untrusted input reaches a DOM HTML sink | HIGH | Taint (dataflow) | A03:2021 Injection |
| `dom/postmessage-origin-missing` | window "message" handler trusts data without checking origin | MEDIUM | Semantic | A08:2021 Software and Data Integrity Failures |
| `crypto/insecure-randomness` | Security token built from Math.random() | HIGH | Heuristic (advisory) | A02:2021 Cryptographic Failures |
| `crypto/non-constant-time-compare` | Secret compared with a non-constant-time operator | HIGH | Heuristic (advisory) | A02:2021 Cryptographic Failures |
| `crypto/weak-hash` | Weak hash algorithm (MD5/SHA-1) | HIGH | Heuristic (advisory) | A02:2021 Cryptographic Failures |
| `csrf/missing-origin-check` | Cookie-authenticated mutation lacks an Origin/CSRF check | HIGH | Heuristic (advisory) | A01:2021 Broken Access Control |
| `secrets/committed-literal` | Hard-coded secret in committed source | HIGH | Heuristic (advisory) | A05:2021 Security Misconfiguration |
| `injection/code` | Untrusted input reaches dynamic code execution | BLOCKER | Taint (dataflow) | A03:2021 Injection |
| `injection/command` | Untrusted input reaches a shell command | BLOCKER | Taint (dataflow) | A03:2021 Injection |
| `injection/path-traversal` | Untrusted input builds a filesystem path | HIGH | Taint (dataflow) | A01:2021 Broken Access Control |
| `injection/sql` | Untrusted input reaches a SQL query | BLOCKER | Taint (dataflow) | A03:2021 Injection |
| `redirect/open-redirect` | Redirect to an untrusted target | MEDIUM | Taint (dataflow) | A01:2021 Broken Access Control |
| `sanitize/incomplete-escape` | Backslash-escaping sanitizer that does not escape the backslash first | HIGH | Taint (dataflow) | A03:2021 Injection |
| `ratelimit/missing-on-ai-route` | AI/LLM route has no rate limit | HIGH | Heuristic (advisory) | A04:2021 Insecure Design |
| `redos/quadratic-regex` | Untrusted input matched against a quadratic regex (ReDoS) | MEDIUM | Semantic | A06:2021 Vulnerable and Outdated Components |
| `redos/super-linear-regex` | Untrusted input matched against a catastrophic regex (ReDoS) | HIGH | Semantic | A06:2021 Vulnerable and Outdated Components |
| `env/public-secret` | Secret exposed via NEXT_PUBLIC_ prefix | BLOCKER | Heuristic (advisory) | A05:2021 Security Misconfiguration |
| `env/secret-in-client` | Server secret read in client-reachable code | BLOCKER | Heuristic (advisory) | A01:2021 Broken Access Control |
| `headers/missing-security-headers` | No security headers emitted | HIGH | Heuristic (advisory) | A05:2021 Security Misconfiguration |
| `ssrf/tainted-fetch` | Server-side request to an untrusted URL (SSRF) | HIGH | Taint (dataflow) | A10:2021 Server-Side Request Forgery |
| `rls/anon-table-grant` | Table granted to anon/public | MEDIUM | Semantic | A01:2021 Broken Access Control |
| `rls/anon-writable` | Anonymous role can modify or delete existing rows | HIGH | Semantic | A01:2021 Broken Access Control |
| `rls/permissive-write-policy` | Write policy with an unconditional predicate | MEDIUM | Semantic | A01:2021 Broken Access Control |
| `rls/policy-not-owner-scoped` | RLS policy authenticates the caller but does not scope rows to them | HIGH | Semantic | A01:2021 Broken Access Control |
| `rls/security-definer-search-path` | SECURITY DEFINER function without a pinned search_path | HIGH | Semantic | A01:2021 Broken Access Control |
| `rls/table-without-rls` | Public table without Row Level Security | HIGH | Semantic | A01:2021 Broken Access Control |
| `rls/write-policy-without-check` | Write policy without WITH CHECK | HIGH | Semantic | A01:2021 Broken Access Control |
| `supabase/service-role-outside-admin` | Supabase service_role key reachable from the client/edge | BLOCKER | Heuristic (advisory) | A01:2021 Broken Access Control |

## Dynamic confirmation (DAST · `@aegiskit/dast`)

Aegis can probe a running app to **confirm** static findings at runtime — a matched dynamic result upgrades
the correlated static finding's confidence to *high* and attaches the HTTP exchange. Probes: reflected XSS,
SQL injection, open redirect, SSRF (out-of-band canary), missing security headers, error disclosure,
rate-limit burst, and — with supplied test identities — auth-required and IDOR. All probes are
non-destructive by default, off-origin requests are blocked, and a request budget bounds every run.

## What Aegis deliberately does NOT detect

These are out of scope **by design** (fail-secure: Aegis prefers a false negative to a false positive).
For each, use the noted complement:

- **Cross-file / multi-hop data flows.** Taint analysis is intraprocedural with depth-1 helper resolution;
  a flow that crosses several modules is not tracked. → Pair with a deep SAST (Semgrep, CodeQL) for
  whole-program flows.
- **Business-logic & workflow flaws** (price tampering, broken state machines, multi-step bypasses). No
  static tool infers intent. → Threat modeling + manual review + targeted DAST.
- **Authorization correctness in the general case.** Aegis proves request-scoped IDOR (`authz/idor-tainted-scope`)
  and Supabase RLS owner-scoping (`rls/*`) precisely, and flags "no visible auth check" as an advisory
  prompt — but it cannot decide arbitrary authorization logic. → Manual review of access decisions.
- **Runtime-only issues** (misconfigured production headers, live auth bypass) unless you run the DAST
  probes against a deployed instance.
- **Dependency / supply-chain CVEs and license risk.** → `npm/pnpm audit`, OSV, Socket, Dependabot.
- **Infrastructure, container, and cloud posture.** → Dedicated CSPM/IaC scanners.

Aegis automates the common, high-impact controls and **complements** secure design — it does not replace it.

---
*Generated from `packages/scanner/src/rules` + `packages/scanner/src/sql-rules` via
`packages/scanner/bench/coverage.ts`. To change this page, change a rule (or its classification) and run
`pnpm --filter @aegiskit/scanner coverage:matrix:write`.*
